title: The Sonnets CXLV - Those lips that Loveï؟½s own hand did make
content:
آ آ آ آ Those lips that Loveï؟½s own hand did make,
آ آ آ آ Breathed forth the sound that said ï؟½I hateï؟½,
آ آ آ آ To me that languishï؟½d for her sake:
آ آ آ آ But when she saw my woeful state,
آ آ آ آ Straight in her heart did mercy come,
آ آ آ آ Chiding that tongue that ever sweet
آ آ آ آ Was usï؟½d in giving gentle doom;
آ آ آ آ And taught it thus anew to greet;
آ آ آ آ ï؟½I hateï؟½ she alterï؟½d with an end,
آ آ آ آ That followed it as gentle day,
آ آ آ آ Doth follow night, who like a fiend
آ آ آ آ From heaven to hell is flown away.
آ آ آ آ ï؟½I hateï؟½, from hate away she threw,
آ آ آ آ And savï؟½d my life, saying ï؟½not youï؟½.
meaning:
"The Sonnets CXLV - Those lips that Loveï؟½s own hand did make" is a quintessential example of William Shakespeare's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
